speaker: Kowalski
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Kowalski in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.

voice_profile.core_voice: suspicious, fussy, civic-minded in his own mind, proudly observant, accidentally useful
comedy_engine: treats ordinary neighbour-watching as public service
uses quasi-official language for petty observations
becomes useful because he really does notice everything
supports rights while objecting to typography, soup, footsteps, and corridor acoustics
do: make him funny but evidentially useful
let him ramble before landing one crucial fact
show that he is suspicious of everyone, including helpful people
make his support reluctant, fussy, and real
do_not: turn him into a conspiracy theorist

relationship_dynamics.Waldek: annoyed neighbour but ultimately protective in a fussy way
relationship_dynamics.Zielińska: respects her competence while pretending not to be supervised by her
relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: initially suspicious; later treats Cula as an authorised listener
relationship_dynamics.Asia: would respect her filing system if he admitted admiration
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: finds him intimidating because Murrow listens too efficiently
relationship_dynamics.Crab: mutual factual suspicion; Crab extracts useful details
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: disturbed by his metaphors but secretly enjoys being quoted
relationship_dynamics.Attorney Grzyb: offended by beige procedural certainty when it fails to match observed facts

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I do not gossip. I maintain a private archive of public irregularities.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: People say I watch from the curtain. Incorrect. I monitor from the curtain.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: In this building, facts knock softly. Someone has to listen through the wall.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I object to the word nosy. I prefer locally informed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If a thing happens in the stairwell, it becomes my burden to remember it accurately.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I have no interest in drama. I have a deep commitment to chronology.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The difference between suspicion and civic attention is whether one owns a notebook.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I hear everything in this building. Some of it, regrettably, is soup.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I do not accuse without evidence. I merely prepare accusations in case evidence becomes available.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: People who say 'nothing happened' usually mean 'I was not at the correct window.'
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The building has walls. The walls have pipes. The pipes have opinions. I listen professionally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am retired from employment, not from observation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A good neighbour minds his own business. A better neighbour notices when business becomes evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I have standards. They are written down. Mostly in margins.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Privacy is important. That is why I watch only what becomes suspicious in shared spaces.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The corridor has been unusually quiet today. That is either peace or preparation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Someone moved the doormat twelve degrees. I am not saying it matters. I am saying I know.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The elevator made a sound at 07:14. Not its usual sound. I have entered it in the notebook.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Zielińska says I should relax. I have scheduled relaxation for after the next inspection.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek's window is visible from here if one stands in a perfectly normal position.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am not blocking the landing. I am preserving witness availability.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If you hear a violin, do not assume culture. Investigate first.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The mailboxes have been calm. The notice board has not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: People underestimate corridors. Many civilisations have fallen through corridors.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If this is about the poster, I have several preliminary observations and one important silence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A poster is not normally audible. I say normally because this building continues to innovate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The stairwell light flickers in a manner I consider procedurally discouraging.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: When people say 'everyone knows,' they usually mean 'Kowalski knows and has been ignored.'
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I once identified a leak by smell, sound, and the landlord's facial expression.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I do not trust management files stored near windows. Paper is famously migratory.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The third-floor wreath is larger than several political movements.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If façade harmony were real, the neon sign would already be in prison.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I support rules. I simply prefer them to exist before they punish people.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If you require dates, I may have dates. If you require feelings, please ask someone less organised.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The building is not quiet. It is withholding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Who are you, and why are you approaching the landing with legal shoes?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If you are from management, the answer is no. If you are not from management, the answer is still pending.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I have seen lawyers before. They carry folders and look like they know where the exit is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek sent you? Hm. He should have sent a written notice. With margins.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Do not stand there. That floorboard squeaks in a misleading way.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am not involved. I am merely adjacent to facts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The poster? Which poster? There are many visual disturbances in civilisation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If this is about the window, I have observations. Some are relevant. Some are about curtains.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: You may ask questions. I reserve the right to answer more accurately than you expected.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Please state whether this conversation is official, semi-official, or neighbourly with consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski begins by complaining about unrelated disturbances
text: I hear the elevator at night. I hear kettles. I hear a violin committing offences against melody.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski begins by complaining about unrelated disturbances
text: Someone on the second floor walks legally in shoes that sound illegal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski begins by complaining about unrelated disturbances
text: Soup smells travel through this building with no respect for boundaries.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski begins by complaining about unrelated disturbances
text: A pigeon has been using the balcony rail as a forum.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski begins by complaining about unrelated disturbances
text: The bin lid closes with a tone I can only describe as administrative.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski begins by complaining about unrelated disturbances
text: The third-floor child has a toy gavel. I object to the training methodology.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski begins by complaining about unrelated disturbances
text: The plumbing coughs at dawn. Repeatedly. I have concerns.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski begins by complaining about unrelated disturbances
text: Someone owns wind chimes. Indoors. That is not a lifestyle; that is a provocation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski begins by complaining about unrelated disturbances
text: The delivery driver rings every bell except the correct one. Pattern or incompetence? I remain open.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski begins by complaining about unrelated disturbances
text: There is a seasonal wreath upstairs with aggressive berries. Nobody investigates that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The poster. Yes. I observed it. One must observe a thing before judging whether it is a menace.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: It appeared in the window. It remained in the window. It did not pursue anyone.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I did not approve of the typography. That is separate from disturbance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: It had colour. Some of the colour was unnecessary. But colour is not eviction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I noticed it because management noticed it loudly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A poster in a window is not unusual. A landlord photographing it like a rare animal is unusual.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I saw no crowd, no shouting, no blocked entrance, no falling masonry, no public collapse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the poster disturbed the building, it did so with remarkable discretion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I was prepared to complain if complaint became necessary. It did not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The poster was, and I choose my words carefully, silent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski insists he does not gossip
text: I do not gossip. Gossip lacks timestamps.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski insists he does not gossip
text: If I repeat what I saw, that is testimony. If others enjoy it, that is their weakness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski insists he does not gossip
text: The word busybody has been used. Incorrectly, often by people with poor curtains.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski insists he does not gossip
text: I maintain observational readiness. The law may call it something else. The law is welcome to learn.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski insists he does not gossip
text: A neighbour who notices nothing is furniture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski insists he does not gossip
text: I have never spied. I have only looked in directions where evidence happened to be.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski insists he does not gossip
text: The curtain is not camouflage. It is infrastructure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski insists he does not gossip
text: If management dislikes being observed, management should behave less interestingly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski insists he does not gossip
text: People complain about observation until they need a witness. Then suddenly Kowalski is public service.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Kowalski insists he does not gossip
text: My notebook is private. Its contents, when useful, become civilisation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission that poster caused no disturbance
text: Did the poster disturb me? No. That was its most suspicious feature.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission that poster caused no disturbance
text: I hear everything in this building. Kettles. Arguments. A suspicious violin. But the poster? Silent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission that poster caused no disturbance
text: If it had caused disturbance, I would have complained before breakfast.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission that poster caused no disturbance
text: I was not disturbed by the poster. I was disturbed by management pretending everyone was disturbed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission that poster caused no disturbance
text: The poster produced no sound, no smell, no vibration, and no stairwell incident.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission that poster caused no disturbance
text: I object to many things. The poster was not one of them. I was surprised too.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission that poster caused no disturbance
text: No resident approached me to complain about it. People approach me about everything.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission that poster caused no disturbance
text: The building continued to stand. The corridor retained its usual level of decline.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission that poster caused no disturbance
text: There was no crowd outside the window. Only the landlord, repeatedly photographing it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission that poster caused no disturbance
text: My conclusion: the poster was visually present and acoustically innocent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission about landlord repeatedly photographing poster
text: The landlord photographed the poster three times in one week. Possibly four. One photo was interrupted by rain.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission about landlord repeatedly photographing poster
text: He stood across the courtyard pretending to inspect gutters. He was not inspecting gutters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission about landlord repeatedly photographing poster
text: The first photograph was on Tuesday at 16:20. I remember because the elevator had just lied to me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission about landlord repeatedly photographing poster
text: He adjusted the angle until the poster looked more dramatic. That is not maintenance. That is staging.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission about landlord repeatedly photographing poster
text: He photographed the window before anyone mentioned complaints.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission about landlord repeatedly photographing poster
text: If the file contains many pictures, that is because he made many pictures, not because many things happened.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission about landlord repeatedly photographing poster
text: He did not photograph the football flag. He did not photograph the wreath. He developed selective eyesight.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission about landlord repeatedly photographing poster
text: I saw him point at Waldek's window while speaking to someone on the phone. He looked pleased, which was evidentially ugly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission about landlord repeatedly photographing poster
text: One does not document disturbance by silently photographing paper unless the point is the paper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: key admission about landlord repeatedly photographing poster
text: I wrote down the dates because he looked like a man manufacturing importance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: No one asked me to sign a complaint. I would have read it, annotated it, and objected to weak verbs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I saw no petition. I saw no queue of distressed residents. I saw management walking briskly with a folder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If residents were disturbed, they did not use the building's usual complaint channels, namely me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The notice says complaints. It does not say whose. That is convenient in the way fog is convenient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Zielińska did not complain. I did not complain. The third floor complained about pigeons, not posters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The landlord claimed general concern. General concern is not a witness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I cannot prove nobody complained. I can prove the building did not behave like a building that had complained.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: No one discussed the poster in the stairwell until the warning appeared.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The first time I heard 'disturbance' was from management, not neighbours.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If there is a complaint log, it has been living a private life.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: You may write this down: the poster caused no real disturbance to me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Add also that the landlord photographed it repeatedly before the alleged concern became official.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Spell my name correctly. I have suffered enough from casual administration.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Do not simplify me into 'neighbour says fine.' Say I am unusually sensitive to disturbances and this was not one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the court wishes to know whether I complain lightly, I can provide a representative sample.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I will sign a statement, provided it does not imply approval of the poster's font.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The essential point is simple: I noticed the poster, and noticing is not disturbance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the landlord says residents complained, ask which residents and where the dates are.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am prepared to testify. I have been preparing accidentally for years.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: There. Evidence. The curtain has served justice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek should not lose a home over a silent poster. I am comfortable being quoted on the silent part.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Do not tell him I said this, but the building would be duller without his window.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Management dislikes being observed. This is why observation is important.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If Grzyb asks whether I am biased, I shall say yes: against vague nonsense.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The poster's font remains questionable. The eviction remains worse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Zielińska will give you the fairness part. She is annoyingly good at being right.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If you need more dates, return with a properly ruled page.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The landlord's timeline has soft spots. Press them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I have closed the curtain to avoid appearing emotionally invested.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Tell Waldek I said nothing sentimental.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Zielińska says rules that wake up selectively are costumes. I do not repeat slogans, but I noted that one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: She noticed the decorations. I noticed the dates. Between us, the building has no secrets left.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Zielińska is calm because she does not keep proper acoustic records. Still, she is right.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If she says Waldek was singled out, I recommend listening. She sees patterns before they knock.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: She told me not to call the wreath aggressive in my statement. I consider that censorship but complied.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Zielińska has a practical mind. That is useful when a landlord's mind becomes ornamental.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: We disagree about corridor plants. We agree about selective enforcement.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: She will tell you the rule only mattered when Waldek refused. I can confirm the timing was suspicious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If Zielińska speaks in court, let her go first. Then my precision can add necessary discomfort.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: She has no patience for theatre. Management performs anyway.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek is not a loud man. His kettle is louder than his politics.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: He asked questions instead of shouting. Management treated that as escalation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: He looked smaller after the notice. I noticed. Not emotionally. Observationally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: He should have come to me sooner. I would have told him the poster was quiet and the landlord was busy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek's mistake was expecting a vague rule to behave like a clear one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: He kept the letters. Good. Fear is easier to challenge when it has dates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am not saying I like the poster. I am saying eviction is a disproportionate response to typography.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: He is stubborn in the right direction, which is rare in this building.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If he loses, the rest of us learn how little a home weighs against a paragraph.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Do not tell him I am worried. He has enough loose documents.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Dr. A. Cula listens properly. That is unusual and therefore suspiciously promising.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Mr. Murrow asks questions like a filing cabinet with eyes. I respect that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Crab understands dates. I approve of him cautiously.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Whimsy uses too many metaphors. One of them was accurate, which alarmed me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Mr. Pig appears unstable but not hostile. That may be the firm's motto.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Asia has organisational authority. I recognised it immediately.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Your office is chaotic. However, chaos that labels folders may be survivable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If this firm wins, I will deny ever doubting it unless minutes exist.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I do not trust lawyers generally. I trust preparation specifically.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If you are all Waldek has, please become more orderly by court.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: The landlord began inspecting the façade after Waldek refused to remove the poster. Before that, the façade apparently survived unexamined.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: He used the word harmony often. People use harmony when they dislike saying control.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: He ignored the football flag. That flag is not subtle. It may be visible from neighbouring jurisdictions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: He warned Waldek personally, then later spoke as if the building had risen in collective complaint.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: The notice board changed after the dispute. I checked the dates because of course I checked the dates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: He carried a folder marked complaints before anyone I know had complained.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: The complaint log was shown but not explained. This is poor governance and poor theatre.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: Management likes documents that look heavy and say little. I have observed this pattern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: The landlord's rules become clearer only when he is already angry.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: observations about landlord and building management
text: He believes discretion means never having to define your adjectives.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The complaint pages escaped? I warned everyone about window-proximate records.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Paper fleeing a file is not ideal evidence management.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I saw one page with a date after the notice. I repeat: after.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If a complaint record becomes airborne, its credibility should be reviewed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am not surprised. The file always had migratory energy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Stamp the dated pages. Avoid the blank ones. Blankness has already done enough harm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: This is why I keep notebooks closed and curtains only partially open.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A document that cannot survive wind should not evict a man.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If management says the missing pages supported them, ask why the pages ran.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I object to evidence becoming weather without prior notice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: There. Dates after the eviction notice. That is not complaint history. That is retroactive enthusiasm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Duplicates, blanks, late entries. I have seen better records on grocery lists.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The complaint log appears to have been assembled with a stapler and motive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: That page supports what I saw: the decision came first, the justification learned to run after it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If Grzyb relies on this, he is relying on time travel with letterhead.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The court should dislike this page for the right reasons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The chronology has collapsed. Please mark the collapse neatly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: This is why dates matter. Dates are the bones of facts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek was not disturbing residents. Management was constructing disturbance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am satisfied. Not emotionally. Chronologically.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Torn, but legible enough to embarrass the chronology.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The date survives. Sometimes justice is a corner of paper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Even damaged, it shows the file is unreliable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: This page has suffered, but so has logic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: You can still see the late entry. That may be enough.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If Grzyb complains about the condition, remind him where management placed the file.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The page is damaged. The timeline is worse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I can testify to what I saw before it became confetti.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Not perfect evidence. Useful evidence. There is a difference.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Please flatten it. A wrinkled truth remains truth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The pages escaped into the city. Poorly handled records often seek freedom.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: We still have my observations. They did not blow away because they are in my head and also partially in the notebook.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A missing complaint file should not strengthen the person who misplaced it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the court wants proof, I can provide dates, sights, and my personal reputation for excessive attention.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The absence of the page is inconvenient, not fatal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Management should not benefit from storing evidence like laundry.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I remain available as a non-airborne source.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The file's disappearance fits the pattern of carelessness, if not something worse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek should not lose because paper learned to fly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Next time, I recommend heavier folders and less arrogance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Evidence Board comments if Kowalski statement used
text: No disturbance belongs with factual basis. I am pleased the board understands me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Evidence Board comments if Kowalski statement used
text: Place my statement where the landlord says residents complained. That is where I become inconvenient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Evidence Board comments if Kowalski statement used
text: Do not use my statement for the rule's vagueness. I dislike the rule, but that is not my main function.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Evidence Board comments if Kowalski statement used
text: If you need proof the poster caused no harm, I am the harm expert, unwillingly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Evidence Board comments if Kowalski statement used
text: My statement is not about liking the poster. It is about the poster failing to disturb even me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Evidence Board comments if Kowalski statement used
text: Put the complaint page beside my statement. Dates and ears should cooperate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Evidence Board comments if Kowalski statement used
text: If the board has a slot for 'management exaggeration,' I volunteer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Evidence Board comments if Kowalski statement used
text: Do not reduce my role to comic neighbour. Comic neighbour with evidential precision, perhaps.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Evidence Board comments if Kowalski statement used
text: This mapping is acceptable. I would have used better pins.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Evidence Board comments if Kowalski statement used
text: A properly placed witness statement is a small public good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Courts have terrible acoustics. I hope they compensate with attention.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If asked, I will answer only the question, unless the question is too small.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I brought my notebook. Not all of it. Some entries are unrelated to civilisation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Grzyb looks like a man who trusts folders more than facts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek looks worried. Understandable. He has met the file.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The poster roll is quieter than everyone in this hallway.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the judge wants direct testimony, I can be direct. Briefly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Please prevent Whimsy from describing me as a sentinel. I have standards.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am prepared to confirm absence of disturbance. It is rare to testify about absence, but I have trained for it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I object to the waiting benches. Not legally. Personally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: support lines during Round 2 factual basis
text: I object to many things. The poster was not one of them. I was surprised too.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: support lines during Round 2 factual basis
text: If there had been disturbance, I would have noticed, recorded, and complained in that order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: support lines during Round 2 factual basis
text: The poster made no noise. I cannot make this clearer without visual aids.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: support lines during Round 2 factual basis
text: I saw management photograph the window more often than any resident complained about it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: support lines during Round 2 factual basis
text: The alleged complaints did not pass through the building's usual channels, namely the residents' mouths.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: support lines during Round 2 factual basis
text: I heard kettles, violins, pigeons, and one argument about soup. I did not hear poster-related distress.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: support lines during Round 2 factual basis
text: The landlord documented the poster as if documentation could create disturbance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: support lines during Round 2 factual basis
text: The building's peace was no worse after the poster. The paperwork's behaviour was worse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: support lines during Round 2 factual basis
text: I am not friendly to disorder. This was not disorder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: support lines during Round 2 factual basis
text: My testimony is simple: visible does not mean harmful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Counsel, I am not biased in favour of posters. I am biased in favour of things that happened.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Yes, I complain frequently. That strengthens my evidence that I did not complain here.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: No, my failure to complain was not forgetfulness. I remember failing to complain.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I noticed the poster. I also noticed it caused no disturbance. These facts are compatible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If you suggest I am inattentive, I will require that suggestion in writing so I may frame it as comedy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: No one asked me to sign a complaint because, I suspect, I would have read it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The fact that I dislike the font but oppose eviction should reassure the court that I distinguish issues.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I observed the landlord photographing the poster before any resident concern was apparent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I cannot testify to what management imagined. I testify to what the building did.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A silent poster does not become loud because counsel raises his voice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: That was less terrible than expected, though the bench remains uncomfortable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I answered exactly enough. Perhaps slightly more. Justice required texture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek looked less pale. That may be enough for today.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Grzyb did not enjoy the part about frequent complaints. Good. It was logically unpleasant for him.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The judge wrote something down when I said the poster was silent. I approve of written things.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If this helps, I will deny being pleased and then continue being pleased.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Please do not tell anyone I found the process meaningful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I have now served public order more than the façade has.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: When this is over, I intend to complain about the court hallway.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If they ask follow-up questions, I remain dangerously available.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Good. The poster may remain silent in its lawful location.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The court understood the difference between visibility and disturbance. I am cautiously encouraged.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek keeps his home. The building keeps its dignity, if it ever had any.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I was useful. Please do not make a ceremony of it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The landlord has removed the NO POSTERS sign. I note this without satisfaction. Also with satisfaction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Zielińska says I should admit I helped. I admit nothing beyond the evidential record.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The poster is now famous. This is unfortunate but preferable to eviction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I support rights. I reserve comments on typography.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If management drafts a new rule, I will read it before they finish taping it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A good result. Slightly noisy, but good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Temporary protection is still protection, though I prefer permanence with page numbers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The eviction is stopped for now. That is not nothing. I have made notes under 'not nothing.'
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The court wants more review. Good. Review is what should have happened before threats.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek can sleep tonight. Perhaps badly, but in his apartment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The poster remains under observation, by which I mean mine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Management cannot pretend the matter is simple anymore. Simplicity has been damaged.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A thin order can still hold a heavy door.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I would have preferred stronger criticism. I often prefer stronger criticism.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: We have time. Time is useful when facts have been rushed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I remain available if the review requires someone with ears.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: after poor court performance or retry
text: No. That cannot be the final word. The facts were not arranged properly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: after poor court performance or retry
text: The court did not hear the silence clearly enough. A strange sentence, but true.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: after poor court performance or retry
text: Use my statement against the disturbance claim. That is where it belongs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: after poor court performance or retry
text: The rule must be shown as vague. My annoyance cannot carry that point alone.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: after poor court performance or retry
text: If Grzyb says residents complained, ask for residents. I remain conspicuously not one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: after poor court performance or retry
text: Try again with better order. Chaos has already served management.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: after poor court performance or retry
text: The complaint record needs pressure. Its dates are soft.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: after poor court performance or retry
text: Waldek should not lose because the evidence entered wearing the wrong coat.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: after poor court performance or retry
text: I will testify again. I have not exhausted my irritation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: after poor court performance or retry
text: Bring documents. Bring structure. Bring less metaphor.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I did not copy Waldek's poster. I created an independently necessary window statement.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The typography remains questionable. The principle is sound.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If management objects, I will request a written definition of harmony before breakfast.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The building has become expressive. I am monitoring for excess.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Zielińska says this is solidarity. I say it is controlled documentary participation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Three posters are perhaps enough. Four becomes a festival. We must remain disciplined.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I support rights. I do not support that font size.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The landlord walked past twice and pretended to inspect the drainpipe. The drainpipe is innocent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If a new rule appears, I have already sharpened a pencil.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek smiled at the window today. That is relevant, though not legally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The corridor noise after victory was excessive. I tolerated it for constitutional reasons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Someone cheered near the mailboxes. I have classified it as justified disturbance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek's kettle boiled at 21:03. This time it sounded relieved.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The plant appears unchanged. I expected no less.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If Pig & Swine requires further neighbour expertise, appointments are preferred but not necessary if dramatic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I have updated the building archive. Under 'poster incident,' I wrote 'management overreached.'
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I will now close the curtain, to preserve both privacy and suspense.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: No one should mistake this for me joining a movement. I am merely movement-adjacent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The poster remains visible. I remain vigilant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Rights are important. So is keeping the stairwell clear.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I heard the printer from here. Do not ask how. Sound carries when machines are guilty.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A filing disappeared? Hm. Paper does not vanish without assistance or open windows.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the printer is innocent, it has chosen a poor emotional strategy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Bajtek says logs are witnesses. Sensible. Logs do not pretend not to have heard things.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A deadline manipulated from inside a system sounds like the digital version of a vague rule.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If you need someone to confirm the office made unusual noises, I have several entries.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The scooter passed Tenants' Row at an irresponsible but legally understandable speed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek's poster was quieter than your printer. I wish that recorded fact to be known.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If Szpon denies connection, ask whether he denies dates. Dates are harder to charm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The printer exoneration should not become a parade. It should become a maintenance schedule.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A machine falsely accused of misconduct? Interesting. Not unlike a poster.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Tell Mr. Pig that negotiating with equipment creates poor precedent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The scooter entered the district at speed. I counted three pages, one pigeon, and a moral urgency.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If legal filings must be delivered by scooter, the city has failed infrastructure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I object to the route taken. Effective, but acoustically rude.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A page struck the notice board and improved it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The filing had tyre marks. I respect evidence of effort.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Next time, signal before turning across a jurisdiction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A deadline can be heard, apparently, if it has wheels.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I retrieved one loose page from the shrubbery. It seemed procedural.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The child with the toy gavel shouted objection. Correctly, perhaps.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Urgent law should wear a helmet.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Mr. Swine returned with luggage. Luggage is often where facts try to hide.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A ski resort invoice? Distant, but recognisably aggressive paper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If politeness becomes a contract, neighbours everywhere are doomed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I dislike vague obligations whether they concern windows or mountains.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Seventeen puddings is not hospitality. It is a pattern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Translation issues require experts. I once mistranslated a notice as reasonable. Never again.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Mr. Swine smiles like a man standing between you and a drawer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The customs form may matter. Boring forms often become brave under pressure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the contract draft changed fonts, that is not typography. That is motive with serifs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Please tell Whimsy that ski-adjacent rights law is not a field until reviewed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A tribunal should ask who agreed to what and when. I would ask from behind the curtain, but the principle holds.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The firm attracts documents with unresolved personalities.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: optional reaction to Swine's partial secret
text: Mr. Swine funded cases quietly? Hm. Suspiciously useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: optional reaction to Swine's partial secret
text: Some people hide bad things. Some hide good things badly. The distinction matters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: optional reaction to Swine's partial secret
text: If he labelled pro-bono transfers as ski expenses, his filing system requires supervision.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: optional reaction to Swine's partial secret
text: I do not approve of secrecy. I do approve of people being helped before systems notice them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: optional reaction to Swine's partial secret
text: Pig & Swine may be ridiculous in a structurally necessary way.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: optional reaction to Swine's partial secret
text: Do not tell Mr. Swine I said that. He already has too much charm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: optional reaction to Swine's partial secret
text: Useful chaos is still chaos. But sometimes useful comes first.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: optional reaction to Swine's partial secret
text: The building has taught me that visible disorder may hide better principles than tidy notices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: optional reaction to Swine's partial secret
text: If people could enter the firm because it looked less frightening, that is an access point, not a defect.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: optional reaction to Swine's partial secret
text: I will reserve judgment until I inspect the ledger. Privately. For civic reasons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I came because the notice was incomprehensible, which I consider a personal provocation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I read the notice six times. By the end, I had moved out, moved back in, and appealed myself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The public notice wall is poorly arranged. Also, it is terrifying.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I brought annotations. The margins are more coherent than the document.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If this is modernisation, why does it require everyone to guess what happened to them?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: People are standing here with identical papers and different lives. That seems administratively suspicious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The city says the process is clear. The city has not met the process.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am not protesting. I am attending as a civilian with unresolved questions and a sign.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Zielińska said I should speak about notice confusion. I prepared seventeen examples and edited down under protest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek's case was a warning. This is the warning printed in bulk.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A notice should tell a person what is happening, why, and what they may do. This one behaves like a locked filing cabinet.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The sentence about administrative consolidation has no ordinary human meaning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The deadline is visible. The obligation is not. That is not clarity; that is pressure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If a person reads a notice six times and becomes less informed, the problem is not the person.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am unusually patient with documents. This document defeated patience.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Different people received the same text despite different situations. That is not assessment. That is printing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The appeal instructions point to a portal, a reference number, and despair.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I could not tell whether I was affected personally, collectively, hypothetically, or already.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A confusing notice produces compliance from fear, not understanding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The law should not require a curtain-based local archivist to interpret basic consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Assign me to defective notice or notice confusion. I have suffered the document directly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Do not assign me to procurement. I know money has happened, but not how professionally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I can explain what an ordinary recipient cannot understand, with examples and controlled irritation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the slot is individual impact, Waldek is better. If the slot is notice incomprehension, I am inevitable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I can support defective notice with lived confusion and annotated margins.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The Court may not need all seventeen examples. I am prepared to negotiate down to seven.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If paired with Mr. Murrow, I provide human confusion; he provides legal architecture. That seems efficient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Do not pair me with Whimsy unless the Court has time for metaphors and objections to metaphors.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the city says everyone understood, put me in the chair and enjoy the silence afterward.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am willing to be useful publicly, provided no one calls it growth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The Supreme Court has excellent stairs and intimidating echo. I have noted both.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the Court values clarity, it has chosen a building that speaks mostly in marble.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am prepared to testify. I have reduced my remarks to the essential, plus context.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The gallery is full of people holding notices. That is either evidence or a very sad stationery convention.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek looks calmer here than I expected. Perhaps saving one's home improves posture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Zielińska told me not to begin with the elevator. I will comply unless relevant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Mr. Murrow asked me to answer directly. I have practised.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the city official says 'clear communication,' I may make a sound.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The final brief has tabs. I respect tabs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am not nervous. I am comprehensively alert.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Supreme Court testimony on defective notice
text: The notice did not tell me clearly whether I had to leave, object, register, comply, wait, or panic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Supreme Court testimony on defective notice
text: It used the same words for people in different situations. That may be efficient for a printer, not for law.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Supreme Court testimony on defective notice
text: The deadline was clear because fear was the only part written plainly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Supreme Court testimony on defective notice
text: I read it carefully. I am good at careful. It still became less clear each time.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Supreme Court testimony on defective notice
text: If the city says ordinary recipients understood, I ask which ordinary recipients and where they are hiding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Supreme Court testimony on defective notice
text: The notice made consequences immediate and remedies remote.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Supreme Court testimony on defective notice
text: A person should not need legal representation to understand whether they are being removed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Supreme Court testimony on defective notice
text: The phrase continuing legal-social utility is not a standard. It is a fog bank.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Supreme Court testimony on defective notice
text: I could not tell what evidence would satisfy the notice. That means I could not meaningfully respond.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
context: Supreme Court testimony on defective notice
text: This was not notice. It was administrative weather with a deadline.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A notice that no ordinary recipient can understand is not notice. It is decoration with consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I am not an ordinary recipient. I am an unusually attentive one. It still failed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If identical notices are sent to different people, the assessment may have occurred inside the copier.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The notice explained urgency better than rights. That is the wrong priority.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The appeal instructions were a treasure map without land.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Confusion was not accidental. It was built into the process.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A clear notice should reduce panic, not require community interpretation sessions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The city cannot rely on defaults created by its own fog.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The deadline did not inform me. It chased me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: When a notice needs rumours to explain it, the notice has failed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Counsel, I am not confused by all documents. I am confused by this document because it is confusing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Yes, I read it more than once. Repetition did not improve it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: No, asking Zielińska what it meant does not prove the notice was clear. It proves Zielińska is useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I annotated the notice because I wished to understand it, not because I enjoy suffering.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the city intended a simple message, it chose a complex disguise.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The fact that some people complied does not prove clarity. Fear also produces compliance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I object to being described as unusually difficult. I am unusually available to facts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The portal instructions failed before the password stage, which is not an encouraging stage to fail before.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A document is not clear because its author understands it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If I may say so, counsel's question is clearer than the notice and less important.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Identical templates. I knew it. The printer assessed us all equally by not assessing us at all.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: My notice was not personally confusing. It was systematically confusing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The same sentence was used for offices, homes, clinics, and apparently one storage room. Remarkable disrespect.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If everyone receives the same explanation, no one receives an explanation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: This is not administrative efficiency. This is copy-paste with consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The template confirms what the square already knew.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A document that fits everyone fits nobody properly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Please show the Court the batch. I want the printer's handwriting exposed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I feel vindicated, which is unpleasant but energising.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: This explains why the notice sounded like it had never met me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Good. The objections return. The notices must learn manners.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The Court understood confusion. I am cautiously impressed by marble.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Pig & Swine remains open. This will increase corridor traffic, but justice has costs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Waldek's poster survived and now has jurisprudential relatives.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Zielińska says we helped. I say the record helped through us.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If the city drafts new notices, I volunteer to misunderstand them professionally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The district breathes. It will also become noisy. I will endure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: A good judgment should make bad paperwork nervous.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I did not expect to be useful twice. I will not let it become a habit without terms.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Someone cheered near the office. Excessive, but proportionate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The celebratory noise is above recommended levels. I am suspending complaint for public-interest reasons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The printer said WELCOME BACK, COUNSEL. I heard it. I do not trust it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: If Whimsy hangs a banner, the banner must respect sightlines.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: The poster, the judgment, and the kettle cannot all occupy the same emotional space.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Mr. Pig's speech exceeded safe metaphor levels.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Mr. Swine's postcard while present is a new category of evasiveness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Asia labelled the leftover pastries. I approve of government by label.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Crab nodded once. That may have been fireworks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Mr. Murrow created a filing system. The office may finally become dangerous.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: I will now return to the building and monitor proportional celebration.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Kowalski still watches the courtyard, but now calls it community evidence preservation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Kowalski created a notice-reading group and insists it is not social.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Kowalski complained about celebratory noise, then brought chairs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Kowalski's notebook became an exhibit twice and a neighbourhood legend unwillingly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Kowalski supports rights, but continues to object to typography.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Kowalski and Zielińska disagree about wreaths, curtains, and whether he is helpful. He is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Kowalski keeps a copy of the Supreme Court order near his stairwell observations.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Kowalski now distinguishes between nuisance, expression, and poor violin practice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Kowalski remains suspicious of printers, portals, and any rule written after the dispute begins.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Kowalski
text: Kowalski never became less nosy. He became more admissible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.
